label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's read the passage carefully. The passage says "One store has three different jars of low fat milk. One says '1 percent fat' on the jar. The second says '99 percent fat free'. The third says 'LOW FAT' in big letters and '1%' in small letters. As you can see, all the milk has the same amount of fat." So the key sentence here is "As you can see, all the milk has the same amount of fat." This directly tells us that all three jars contain the same amount of fat. Therefore, the correct answer should reflect this. The question is asking which jar has a different amount of fat, but according to the passage, they all have the same amount. So the correct choice would be the one that states they all have the same amount of fat. Let me check the options again: (A) one has more fat than the other two (B) they all cost the same amount of money (C) one has less fat than the others (D) they all have the same amount of fat The passage also mentions "However, in this store the three jars of milk cost three different amounts of money." So
